businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' company spanning autonomous driving, robotics, and energy storage
businessSummary.description: Tesla is transitioning from EV manufacturing to an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform. FY2025 revenue $94.8B (-2.9% YoY), net income $3.8B (-46.5% YoY). Tuesday close $399.24 (+0.14%). Rose as high as $405.80 on strong battery supplier earnings before settling flat. VP Finance Sendil Palani departed after 17 years, extending executive exodus concerns. Awaiting NHTSA FSD safety review results after 3/9 deadline. GTC 2026 (3/16-19) approaching with autonomous driving theme resurgence. Market cap $1.50T, P/E 369.66x, Forward P/E 193.06x.
howTheyMakeMoney: Vehicle sales: ~73% of revenue ($69.5B). Model 3/Y core, Model S/X discontinuing Q2 2026
Energy business: ~14% of revenue ($12.8B, +27% YoY). Megapack/Powerwall growth
Services/Other: ~10% of revenue. Supercharger network, insurance, parts
FSD/Software: ~$3B in license revenue. Subscription conversion accelerating

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on EPS $1.08. Extreme premium reflecting autonomous driving/robotics optionality

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026E EPS $2.07. Still elevated but includes recovery expectations

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 sharp decline. Price war + increased R&D spending

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Reflects FY2025 profitability decline. FY2026E improvement expected

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA FSD Safety Review Results Imminent — Watershed for Autonomy Narrative
growthDrivers.description: FSD safety data submission for 2.88M vehicles completed on 3/9. Results could accelerate nationwide autonomous driving expansion or trigger a major recall. Market expects results 'within weeks.'

growthDrivers.title: GTC 2026 (3/16-19) Sparks Autonomous Driving Theme Revival
growthDrivers.description: NVIDIA's next-gen Rubin architecture reveal at GTC could include discussion of next-gen AI chips for Tesla FSD, drawing renewed attention to the entire autonomous driving sector.

growthDrivers.title: Battery Supplier Strong Earnings Signal Tesla Cost Benefits
growthDrivers.description: Key battery supplier's strong earnings drove Tesla shares up 1.8% to $405.80 in early trading. Battery cost reductions could flow through to Tesla margin improvement.

growthDrivers.title: Optimus Gen 3 Robot Production Ramp — New Wright's Law Curve
growthDrivers.description: Analysis suggests Optimus Gen 3 robotics ramp and long-term production efficiency gains could create a new growth curve for TSLA, offsetting near-term vehicle delivery declines.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market leader with 50%+ share, though globally surpassed by BYD. Vertical integration across software, manufacturing, energy, and 8.2B FSD miles is the core moat.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles. Overwhelmingly more training data than competitors.
In-house battery (4680), custom chips (HW4), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS as North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader in 2025. Rapid European growth, US limited by tariffs

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: $16B funding, 400K+ rides per week. Direct autonomous driving competitor

competitors.name: Rivian (RIVN)
competitors.detail: R2 SUV ($45,000) launching 2026, direct Model Y competitor

competitors.name: Xiaomi
competitors.detail: YU7 SUV pre-orders 2:1 vs Model Y in China

recentNews.headline: VP Finance Sendil Palani departs after 17 years — executive exodus grows
recentNews.significance: Departure of a long-tenured senior executive deepens concerns about organizational stability, though immediate stock impact was limited.

recentNews.headline: Tesla shares rise 1.8% on battery supplier's strong earnings
recentNews.significance: Key battery supplier's robust results raised expectations for Tesla cost improvements.

recentNews.headline: NHTSA FSD safety data submission deadline passes — awaiting results
recentNews.significance: Final deadline for 2.88M vehicle FSD safety review. Outcome determines autonomy expansion or recall.

recentNews.headline: Tesla stock tumbles below $400 — regulatory uncertainty + demand weakness
recentNews.significance: FSD regulatory deadline + demand slowdown + tech selloff triple headwinds.

recentNews.headline: German IG Metall union targets Tesla Berlin factory for breakthrough
recentNews.significance: Works council election at Berlin Gigafactory with active union involvement. European labor risk.

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wall Street remains deeply divided. Bulls value autonomous driving and robotics optionality while bears point to EV demand weakness and extreme valuation. Median target $458.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Review Outcome Risk
risks.description: Safety review results for 2.88M vehicles could mean nationwide FSD expansion or a major recall. The entire autonomy narrative hangs in the balance.

risks.title: Accelerating Executive Departures
risks.description: VP Finance Sendil Palani (17 years) departure. Multiple senior executives have left in the past year. Organizational stability concerns.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation
risks.description: P/E 369.66x, Forward P/E 193.06x. PEG 4.10. Excessive premium relative to current earnings.

risks.title: Intensifying Global EV Competition
risks.description: BYD reclaiming global leadership, Xiaomi YU7 pre-order surge in China, Rivian R2 launch imminent.

risks.title: European Labor Risk
risks.description: Berlin Gigafactory facing union influence expansion. IG Metall actively intervening in works council elections.

buyReasons.title: FSD Approval Could Trigger Autonomous Platform Re-rating
buyReasons.rationale: NHTSA approval would accelerate nationwide robotaxi expansion. Austin robotaxi demand remains strong at $3.25 fare, proving monetization potential.

buyReasons.title: Optimus Robot + Energy Business Optionality
buyReasons.rationale: Gen 3 robot ramp and energy business ($12.8B, +27% YoY) diversify growth beyond EV dependency.

buyReasons.title: Net Cash $29.3B + FCF $6.2B Improvement
buyReasons.rationale: Strong financial position. FCF +73.7% YoY growth provides ample investment capacity.

buyReasons.title: Trading Below 50-Day SMA ($424) — RSI 42.64
buyReasons.rationale: Trading at 6% discount to 50-day moving average. RSI near lower neutral zone suggests potential bounce.

overallOpinion: Tesla stands at a 'historic watershed' with the NHTSA FSD review results imminent. Tuesday's $399.24 close (+0.14%) was flat as the stock searches for direction below its 50-day SMA ($424).
The VP Finance departure reignited executive exodus concerns, but strong battery supplier earnings and GTC 2026 anticipation supported the downside.

P/E of 369.66x remains extreme, and without autonomous driving or robotics momentum, current valuation is difficult to justify. A 'wait-and-see' stance is rational until NHTSA results arrive, though both sharp upside and downside scenarios are in play.

threads.hook: Tesla VP Finance exits after 17 years 🚪
threads.text: Stock flat at $399 despite battery supplier boost. The real catalyst? NHTSA FSD review results — approval means nationwide robotaxis, failure means recall on 2.88M vehicles. Can a P/E of 369x survive that? 👉 investory.kro.kr/stock/TSLA/analysis/2026-03-11

telegram.text:
Battery supplier earnings lifted shares to $405 intraday before settling flat. VP Finance Sendil Palani departed after 17 years, adding to executive exodus concerns.

The real story: NHTSA FSD safety review results. After the 3/9 deadline, results expected 'within weeks.' Approval unlocks nationwide robotaxis; failure could mean a 2.88M vehicle recall.

P/E 369x, Forward P/E 193x. With GTC 2026 (3/16) also ahead, this week and next could be inflection points. RSI at 42.6, approaching oversold.

Full analysis 👉 investory.kro.kr/stock/TSLA/analysis/2026-03-11
